draft.title: BP Whiting - Safety Assistant - Analytics (batch 2) - Header & Navigation
draft.summary: This suite batch adds review-ready manual tests focused on the Analytics header/navigation bar for the BP Whiting SafetyOS Dashboard. Coverage emphasizes critical navigation elements (notifications, settings, user/role context, and the visible global date range), interaction safety, accessibility/usability, and regression of header state after filter changes.

assumptions: The header navigation includes clickable icons/links for Notifications and Settings, and shows the current user name and role (e.g., Robert Carter / Safety Director) as shown in the provided mockup.
The header displays the active global date range in the top bar (e.g., '10 MAR 2026 - 17 MAR 2026') and updates it when the shared date range filter changes.
Notifications and Settings open panels/pages (or dropdowns) that can be closed via outside click/Esc or a close icon.
Keyboard focus can be moved into header menus and is returned to the original control when menus close.
The page-level unit includes validation that global filters (date/unit) drive widget reloads and that UI prevents invalid global filter states.
Widget data is API-backed and should show per-widget loaders while fetching.
Filter persistence is stored per user session (e.g., localStorage/sessionStorage).
The mockup represents the visible labels/controls used for validation (e.g., '10 MAR 2026 - 17 MAR 2026', 'ALL UNITS').
The Shared Date Range Picker uses an Apply button to commit selections (as implied by the user story).
The date picker calendar opens as a popup/modal anchored to the top filter bar date input.
The date range format is DD MMM YYYY and the displayed range is updated after Apply.
All dependent widgets (KPI cards, distribution chart, tile grid, trend panel) refresh on shared date range Apply.
Future end dates are capped at today and a tooltip message appears ('Future dates are not selectable').
The product enforces a configurable maximum selectable range with a default of 90 days (exact UI enforcement behavior may vary).
The unit selector is a multi-select dropdown with checkboxes per option and an ALL UNITS option at the top.
The dropdown label changes to a selection count when not all units are selected (e.g., “3 Units Selected”).
At least one unit remains selected at all times (ALL UNITS acts as a default and may re-apply automatically).
Unit selection persists across refresh when persistence is supported (per product pattern).
When unit options API is loading, interaction is disabled or limited to prevent invalid selection.
The KPI card section contains at least four prominent cards: Observations Recorded, Timely Observation Closure, Ratio of High Risk Observation, and SOH%.
Observations Recorded excludes observations marked as false positives from the total (as stated in the provided KPI rule description).
The delta badge includes both an arrow indicator (up/down) and color-coded styling (red for increase, green for decrease) compared against a previous equivalent window shown with a date label.
When filters change, each KPI card independently shows a loading state and then updates with new values.
Empty/no-data and error handling are shown inline within the KPI card area (not as a full page failure).
“Section-Level Date Mode Controls” correspond to a widget section that supports at least two modes: Selected Dates (follows page-level date range) and Custom Dates (uses a section-local date range).
When Custom Dates is active, the section shows its own custom date picker (distinct from the shared/top date picker) and its selected custom range is preserved when toggling modes.

If the custom date picker has an Apply button, it must be used to apply changes to the section’s tiles/counts. If it auto-applies, steps should still validate that the section refreshes only after the stated control interaction.

The observed evidence does not include explicit UI labels for the section-level toggle; the test cases reference common labels from the rule pack (“Selected Dates” / “Custom Dates”) and note any label mismatch in gaps/notes if needed.
The Distribution Summary Chart is driven by the shared global date range picker and shared unit selector.
The donut chart slices correspond to observable risk/observation categories/segments that should map 1:1 with the legend labels.
Hover interactions are supported on desktop (mouse) and should not be disruptive on touch devices; on mobile, tooltip may appear via tap if supported, otherwise hover-only behavior is acceptable.
If the underlying data is malformed or API fails, the UI shows a safe empty/error fallback rather than breaking the chart container.
The Distribution Summary Chart is a donut visualization with interactive slices and an associated legend/side breakdown area visible on the page mockup.
Hovering a donut slice reveals a tooltip with slice label and its count/percentage (exact fields may vary).
The chart supports loading placeholders while API-backed data is fetched after global filter changes.
When the API returns empty/zero distributions, the chart shows a defined empty/no-data state without breaking layout.
When the API response is malformed, the UI degrades gracefully (shows empty/error state) rather than crashing.

Cross-widget reconciliation expectation is that chart totals/segments are consistent with the same scope used by KPI cards and other summary widgets (exact reconciliation method depends on how the donut is computed).
Category tiles in the grid have an expand/collapse control that reveals subordinate segregation breakdown counts.
When expanded, segregation (sub-category/sub-segmentation) counts add back to the parent category total shown on the tile.
The tile grid provides an empty/no-data visual fallback when the selected scope returns no category/category-segregation data.
Category Summary Tile Grid supports expanding a category tile to reveal subordinate segregation counts (as implied by the supporting rule pack).
The tile grid updates in response to shared scope changes (date range + unit selection) and displays a widget-level loading state during refresh.
The tile grid uses a keyboard-focusable expand/collapse control per tile.
The Trend Visualization Tabs offer at least two modes (e.g., Safety Trend and Category Trend) as implied by the mockup section 'Time-wise Trend' with Bar/Line controls.
Tab switching triggers data refresh for line_chart_widget:trend-visualization-panel and clears any stale chart values.
Tab tooltips/hover states are implemented via the chart tooltip system and should not show values from the prior tab mode.
Trend Visualization Panel displays a time-series using either Bar or Line mode controlled by Trend Visualization Tabs.
Hovering line chart points displays an “Insight”/tooltip with the hovered time interval and a calculated metric/value.
No-data and malformed payload behaviors are implemented with safe empty/error UI states rather than crashing.
The trend chart metric is scoped by the same shared date range and unit selector as KPI cards and donut charts.
The Trend Visualization Panel supports a Line mode selected via Trend Visualization Tabs.
Chart hover (mouse) reveals a tooltip; focus/keyboard hover behavior is either present or should be validated as part of accessibility checks.
“Minimal date range” includes the smallest valid range the date picker allows (e.g., single-day selection).
Malformed data can be induced in test environments via controlled backend responses (e.g., invalid/null point values) or API mocking.
Record cards in the carousel represent “high risk observations” qualified by the system’s SIF/high-risk qualification logic.
Carousel includes Previous/Next controls and may include automatic scrolling per rule pack.
Clicking/tapping a carousel record card navigates to an observation detail context (or opens a detail view) aligned with access control.
Carousel content is scoped by the currently applied global date range and selected units.
Carousel cards in BP Whiting Analytics route to an observation detail context (observation review page or detail panel) via click/tap on the card or a dedicated handoff control.
The carousel qualifies and orders records based on a SafetyOS rule (latest qualifying high-risk/SIF/LCI observations).
Prev/Next controls exist and support both mouse and keyboard interactions.

An automatic scrolling behavior may exist (as referenced in the rule pack), but if auto-scroll is not present in the actual UI, the auto-scroll precedence test should be treated as a conditional/skip check.
The Assistant Drawer is toggled via an in-page control (e.g., a lightbulb icon or “Actions Insights” entry) and can be opened/closed without full page navigation.
Assistant suggested questions and insight cards are visible when the drawer opens (if the backend returns them).
The assistant composer has a disabled/enabled send action based on whether a valid prompt is present (per knowledge base).
When global filters change (date range and/or units), the assistant should refresh or clear stale context so that insights/history cannot mismatch the active scope.
If backend calls fail, the drawer shows safe error UI and remains usable (retry or allow future requests).
If access control limits assistant context (e.g., user role), unauthorized content is not displayed and cannot be retrieved via UI actions.
The historical date picker is a single-date calendar control (not a start/end range).
Selecting a historical date triggers refresh of at least one time-scoped visualization on the page (e.g., Time-wise Trend and/or Observation Heat Map).
If persistence of the historical date is not supported, it should revert to a configured default (per the shared date picker patterns in the product).
If the product blocks future dates for this control, it uses a disabled-date approach and/or an inline tooltip consistent with the shared date picker behavior ('Future dates are not selectable').
The Cumulative Date Range Picker includes the same core behaviors as the shared date range picker: calendar popup, Apply workflow, validation, future-date restriction, and session persistence.
The cumulative picker’s selected range drives cumulative-specific widgets (notably the Cumulative Zone-Wise Split Donut Chart).
If the UI labels or exact presence/absence of an 'Apply' button differs from the shared picker, the expected results should be interpreted as the cumulative picker’s equivalent commit action.
The cumulative zone-wise donut chart is visible within the analytics dashboard section that corresponds to cumulative stats.
The tooltip behavior includes both hover and focus/keyboard activation where supported.
The legend shown for this chart is mapped to donut slices by label and color.
Empty data for the donut chart can occur when the selected cumulative range has no high-risk observations or no qualifying zone-split data.
The cumulative zone-wise split donut chart renders zone segments and a side legend with zone labels and color mapping.
Hovering a donut slice displays a tooltip with the corresponding zone label and its value/percentage.
The chart updates when cumulative date range scope is changed (via the Cumulative Date Range Picker).
Some failure modes can be induced in a test environment (e.g., returning empty results, partial buckets, malformed payload, or API error).
The legend entries (and their ordering/colors) correspond to chart segments in the donut charts shown on the page.
Hover/focus interactions on the chart also validate mapping, and legend hover (if present) should not conflict with tooltip content.
If legend hover is not implemented, tooltip checks will be performed via chart segment hover while still validating legend mapping visually.
Donut charts and the trend chart render interactive hover targets that trigger tooltips.
Tooltips also support keyboard focus or are at minimum accessible via keyboard (e.g., focusable SVG elements or chart accessibility layer).
During loading/empty/error states, charts either suppress tooltips or show a safe fallback (no stale tooltip content).
Tooltip content includes a label (category/zone/time bucket) and a value/count/percentage as applicable.
When shared filters change, each widget shows its own loading region rather than only a page-level spinner.
During loading, the UI should not display stale numeric values/old chart segments as if they are current.

testCases.title: Verify that header global context rendering and visibility works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure the Analytics header shows the expected global context information from the dashboard state.
testCases.scenario: Header Global Context Rendering And Visibility
preconditions: User is logged in and can access the BP Whiting Analytics Dashboard
Dashboard page is loaded
testData: Default date range shown in header (example: 10 MAR 2026 - 17 MAR 2026)

steps.action: Open the BP Whiting SafetyOS Dashboard - Analytics page in a desktop browser.
steps.expectedResult: Header is visible at the top and does not overlap the dashboard content.

steps.action: Verify the header displays the user name and role (e.g., 'Robert Carter' and 'Safety Director').
steps.expectedResult: User identity and role text are present and readable.

steps.action: Verify the header displays the active global date range in the top navigation bar (format DD MMM YYYY - DD MMM YYYY).
steps.expectedResult: Date range text is present, formatted as expected, and matches the dashboard filter context.

steps.action: Verify presence of Notifications and Settings entries/icons on the header.
steps.expectedResult: Notifications and Settings controls are visible and appear clickable.

sourceReferences: Source 1 (AnalyticsLatest.html) - header displays user identity/role, notifications/settings, and top-bar date range '10 MAR 2026 - 17 MAR 2026'
testCases.notes: This test focuses on what is visible in the header mockup. It does not require opening panels.

testCases.title: Verify that header interaction does not block chart tooltips hover states works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure header interactions do not interfere with chart tooltip/hover behaviors that depend on shared UI event handling.
testCases.scenario: Header Interaction Does Not Block Chart Tooltips Hover States
preconditions: Dashboard page is loaded
Chart is visible (at least Trend Visualization area and/or any chart with tooltip support)
testData: Attempted future end date selection (e.g., end date > today)

steps.action: Hover over a visible chart element that supports tooltips (e.g., a point/bar in Time-wise Trend) and confirm a tooltip appears.
steps.expectedResult: Tooltip appears with visible label/value.

steps.action: Open the Settings control from the header and close it without navigating away (outside click/Esc).
steps.expectedResult: Settings opens/closes cleanly without breaking the page.

steps.action: Hover the same chart element again immediately after closing Settings.
steps.expectedResult: Tooltip behavior still works; the tooltip content is not missing, incorrect, or stuck.

steps.action: Open the shared date picker and attempt to select a future end date.
steps.expectedResult: Tooltip/message indicates future dates are not selectable, and calendar remains in valid state.

sourceReferences: Source 1 (AnalyticsLatest.html) - chart/tooltip behavior exists and date picker future-date tooltip is described
Source 2 (Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx) - future end date is capped and tooltip 'Future dates are not selectable' is shown
testCases.notes: This is a cross-interaction check: header menus must not interfere with global UI event handling for tooltips/date picker.

testCases.title: Verify that header keyboard accessibility and focus management works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure header controls are keyboard accessible and focus is managed correctly.
testCases.scenario: Header Keyboard Accessibility And Focus Management
preconditions: Dashboard page is loaded
Keyboard-only interaction is possible (no mouse required)
testData: N/A

steps.action: Press Tab repeatedly until the focus reaches the Notifications control in the header.
steps.expectedResult: Focus indicator is visible and the control is reachable via keyboard in a logical order.

steps.action: Press Enter or Space to open the notifications menu/panel.
steps.expectedResult: Menu/panel opens and focus moves into the open overlay/panel (or first actionable element within it).

steps.action: Press Escape to close the notifications menu/panel.
steps.expectedResult: Menu/panel closes and focus returns to the Notifications control.

steps.action: Repeat Steps 1-3 for the Settings control in the header.
steps.expectedResult: Same focus/closure behavior applies to Settings.

sourceReferences: Source 1 (AnalyticsLatest.html) - header contains notifications and settings controls
Source 2 (Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx) - accessibility expectations exist for interactive date/filter behavior; apply same baseline interaction quality to header controls
testCases.notes: If the notifications/settings are not keyboard-activatable in the UI, file as usability/accessibility defect.

testCases.title: Verify that header menu open close interaction safety works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure header menu interactions are isolated and do not corrupt the global filter state.
testCases.scenario: Header Menu Open Close Interaction Safety
preconditions: Dashboard page is loaded
A non-default date range is already applied (optional but recommended to observe accidental resets)
testData: Any valid persisted date range currently applied

steps.action: Record the currently displayed header date range text and the visible Observations Recorded KPI value.
steps.expectedResult: You can clearly read and note the current header date range and KPI count.

steps.action: Click the Notifications control in the header to open its panel/dropdown.
steps.expectedResult: Notifications panel/dropdown opens successfully without blocking page content.

steps.action: Click outside the notifications panel (or press Escape if supported) to close it.
steps.expectedResult: Notifications panel closes and focus returns to the notifications control.

steps.action: Re-check the header date range text and the Observations Recorded KPI value.
steps.expectedResult: Header date range and KPI count remain unchanged (no unintended filter reset).

sourceReferences: Source 1 (AnalyticsLatest.html) - header shows notifications icon/label and date range context
testCases.notes: If Notifications uses a dedicated route/page instead of a panel, adapt steps to back-navigation and re-check date/KPIs.

testCases.title: Verify that header global context updates after date filter apply works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that confirm header reflects applied global date range and that header context remains consistent with persisted filter state.
testCases.scenario: Header Global Context Updates After Date Filter Apply
preconditions: Dashboard page is loaded
Browser supports storage/session persistence
testData: Custom date range: 01 MAR 2026 – 15 MAR 2026

steps.action: Click the shared date range picker in the top filter bar and select start date 01 MAR 2026 and end date 15 MAR 2026.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar highlights the selected range and the selection is ready to apply.

steps.action: Click Apply (or equivalent confirm action).
steps.expectedResult: All widgets show a loading state and then refresh for the new window.

steps.action: Verify the header date range text now matches '01 MAR 2026 - 15 MAR 2026'.
steps.expectedResult: Header date range updates to the applied selection.

steps.action: Refresh the browser page (Ctrl+R).
steps.expectedResult: Header date range remains '01 MAR 2026 - 15 MAR 2026' and the widgets reload with the persisted window.

sourceReferences: Source 1 (AnalyticsLatest.html) - header displays active top-bar date range text
Source 2 (Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx) - date picker applies custom range and persists across refresh
testCases.notes: This validates header-context correctness specifically, not just widget updates.

testCases.title: Verify that default selection behavior works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure the unit dropdown initializes to ALL UNITS and page data scope reflects all authorized units.
testCases.scenario: Default Selection Behavior
preconditions: User is logged in with access to multiple units (not only one).
Analytics dashboard is reachable and page loads successfully.
testData: Default visible date range: 10 MAR 2026 - 17 MAR 2026 (from header).
User access includes at least two units; e.g., Unit 1 and Unit 4 are accessible.

steps.action: Load the Analytics dashboard.
steps.expectedResult: The unit dropdown in the top filter bar displays the label “ALL UNITS” by default.

steps.action: Observe that the page content (e.g., Observations Recorded KPI card and risk distribution) finishes loading.
steps.expectedResult: Widgets display data consistent with an ALL-UNITS scope (aggregated across all units the user can access).

steps.action: Open the unit dropdown.
steps.expectedResult: The dropdown shows an “ALL UNITS” option and individual unit options.

steps.action: Verify that all units shown are only those the user is authorized to view.
steps.expectedResult: Unauthorized units are not present in the dropdown list.

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 2 – Unit Selection Dropdown with Role-Based Access; default is ALL UNITS; multi-select; role-based options)
Source 1: AnalyticsLatest.html (mockup showing “ALL UNITS” and unit dropdown placement)
testCases.notes: Focus is on permission-filtered option visibility and default scope aggregation.

testCases.title: Verify that multi select aggregation behavior works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure multi-select aggregation works and widgets reload consistently for the selected unit set.
testCases.scenario: Multi Select Aggregation Behavior
preconditions: User has access to at least three units.
Analytics dashboard is loaded and initial widgets render.
testData: Select two specific units from dropdown (e.g., Unit 1 and Unit 4).

steps.action: Open the unit dropdown.
steps.expectedResult: A checkbox list of accessible units appears along with “ALL UNITS”.

steps.action: Uncheck “ALL UNITS” if it behaves as select-all, then check Unit 1 and Unit 4.
steps.expectedResult: Both Unit 1 and Unit 4 are selected and any non-selected units are unchecked.

steps.action: Observe the dropdown label.
steps.expectedResult: Label updates to reflect a count-based selection (e.g., “2 Units Selected”).

steps.action: Wait for widgets to refresh.
steps.expectedResult: KPI cards and charts update to reflect data aggregated across only the selected units (Unit 1 + Unit 4).

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 2 – Multi-select Behavior: selected units checked; dropdown label updates to count; dashboard widgets reload filtered by selected units)
testCases.notes: To avoid duplication, do not assert exact KPI numbers—assert scope correctness (units included vs excluded) and synchronized refresh across multiple widgets.

testCases.title: Verify that select all deselect all synchronization behavior works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that confirm Select All / ALL UNITS keeps individual selections aligned without requiring manual checkbox management.
testCases.scenario: Select All Deselect All Synchronization Behavior
preconditions: User has access to multiple units.
testData: Select an initial subset first (e.g., Unit 2 and Unit 3), then return to ALL UNITS.

steps.action: Open the unit dropdown and select two individual units (e.g., Unit 2 and Unit 3) and confirm widgets refresh.
steps.expectedResult: Selected units are checked; dropdown label indicates a count-based selection.

steps.action: Click “ALL UNITS”.
steps.expectedResult: The dropdown label reverts to “ALL UNITS”.

steps.action: Verify checkbox states for individual units in the list.
steps.expectedResult: All accessible individual unit checkboxes become checked.

steps.action: Deselect one individual unit checkbox while ALL UNITS is active (if UI permits).
steps.expectedResult: ALL UNITS and remaining selections synchronize correctly (e.g., ALL UNITS unchecked and count-based label shown) and widgets refresh to reflect the new unit set.

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 2 – Select All / Deselect All and synchronization: ALL UNITS at top; label reverts; all accessible units selected; label changes based on count)
testCases.notes: If the UI does not allow toggling individual checkboxes while ALL UNITS is checked, record as an observed UI behavior and still validate consistent state transitions.

testCases.title: Verify that option visibility via role based access works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that validate access control is enforced at the unit selector level and prevents selecting unauthorized units.
testCases.scenario: Option Visibility Via Role Based Access
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to only Unit A and Unit B (no access to Unit C).
Analytics dashboard loads.
testData: Unauthorized unit example: Unit C (present in system but not in user’s access).

steps.action: Open the unit dropdown.
steps.expectedResult: Only Unit A, Unit B, and “ALL UNITS” are visible as selectable options.

steps.action: Attempt to locate/select Unit C by scanning options and using any in-dropdown search (if present).
steps.expectedResult: Unit C does not appear; it cannot be selected via UI.

steps.action: Select Unit A and Unit B (or leave ALL UNITS selected).
steps.expectedResult: Widgets refresh successfully without any unauthorized data appearing.

steps.action: Refresh the browser and reopen the unit dropdown.
steps.expectedResult: Unauthorized units remain hidden; previously persisted valid selections (if any) remain restricted to authorized units only.

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 2 – Access Control: Unit C and any other units are NOT visible; user cannot manipulate URL/API to retrieve unauthorized units)
testCases.notes: This test is UI-centric; if URL/API manipulation is testable in environment, record that as follow-up.

testCases.title: Verify that mandatory non empty selection behavior works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure user cannot deselect the last remaining unit; dropdown never ends with an empty selection state.
testCases.scenario: Mandatory Non Empty Selection Behavior
preconditions: User has access to at least two units.
testData: Start with a custom selection of exactly two units.

steps.action: Open the unit dropdown and switch from ALL UNITS to selecting exactly two individual units.
steps.expectedResult: Dropdown label shows a count-based state (e.g., “2 Units Selected”).

steps.action: Uncheck one of the two selected units, leaving exactly one unit selected.
steps.expectedResult: One unit remains selected successfully; widgets refresh to reflect that single unit scope.

steps.action: Attempt to uncheck the final remaining selected unit.
steps.expectedResult: The UI prevents reaching zero selected units; selection automatically reverts to a non-empty state (e.g., ALL UNITS or the last unit remains checked), consistent with the mandatory selection rule.

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 2 – Fields & Validations: Mandatory: at least one unit must be selected at all times; Select All / Deselect All behavior)
testCases.notes: Expected behavior may be either disabling the last checkbox toggle or auto-selecting ALL UNITS; record which occurs.

testCases.title: Verify that that that loading state behavior for unit options works correctly works correctly works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that validate that while unit options are loading (options API in-flight), the dropdown does not allow invalid interaction and UI does not desync.
testCases.scenario: Loading State Behavior For Unit Options
preconditions: Ability to simulate slow network/loading for the unit options API (e.g., throttling via browser devtools).
testData: Network throttling profile: Slow 3G (or equivalent).

steps.action: Apply network throttling to delay the Unit options data fetch.
steps.expectedResult: Unit selector may display a loading indicator state or temporarily disable selection until options arrive.

steps.action: Open the unit dropdown during the loading period.
steps.expectedResult: Dropdown does not show stale or partial unit list; selection actions are blocked/disabled or handled safely.

steps.action: Once options finish loading, select a unit and confirm the dropdown closes/updates accordingly.
steps.expectedResult: Selection works and widgets refresh once with consistent scope (no flicker or mixed selections).

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Dependencies: Roles/Units API; dropdown options are limited to user permissions)
Knowledge base: Multiselect standard risks include selection loss on blur and overflow; Loading state should prevent invalid interaction.
testCases.notes: If no explicit loading indicator exists, assert that behavior is still safe (no partial list selection, no stale label/count).

testCases.title: Verify that that that error handling when unit options api fails works correctly works correctly works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure the page behaves safely when the unit options API fails (no broken dropdown, no data scope corruption).
testCases.scenario: Error Handling When Unit Options API Fails
preconditions: Ability to simulate failure for the unit options/roles API (e.g., mock 500/timeout).
User previously loaded the dashboard successfully.
testData: Simulate unit options API returning 500 or timing out.

steps.action: With the dashboard open, trigger unit dropdown open while unit options API is forced to fail.
steps.expectedResult: Dropdown shows a graceful error/empty/disabled state; it does not crash or display unauthorized units.

steps.action: Attempt to change unit selection during the error state.
steps.expectedResult: Invalid selection is prevented; existing selection (if already applied) remains stable and widgets do not switch to an incorrect scope.

steps.action: Restore the API (remove failure) and reopen the dropdown.
steps.expectedResult: Dropdown recovers and displays correct authorized options; the selected scope remains consistent or updates according to product design.

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Dependencies: User Access Control / Roles API; Unit options limited to authorized units)
Knowledge base: Multiselect includes states for loading/error; dropdown should show graceful error or empty state and not break the page.
testCases.notes: Expected exact error text is not specified; validate safe UI state and scope stability.

testCases.title: Verify that state persists correctly after refresh.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure unit multi-select state is retained across refresh and widgets reload with the persisted unit set.
testCases.scenario: State Persistence After Refresh
preconditions: User has access to multiple units.
Unit selection persistence is enabled via session/localStorage per product pattern.
testData: Select two units (e.g., Unit 1 and Unit 4).

steps.action: Open unit dropdown and select exactly two individual units (Unit 1 and Unit 4).
steps.expectedResult: Dropdown label indicates count-based selection (e.g., “2 Units Selected”) and widgets refresh.

steps.action: Refresh the browser (F5/Reload).
steps.expectedResult: Page reloads without losing the unit selection.

steps.action: Observe the unit dropdown selected state and wait for widgets to finish loading.
steps.expectedResult: Selected units remain checked; widgets show data for only those units (no fallback to ALL UNITS unless that is the intended persistence behavior after refresh).

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 2 – Persistence: unit selection is retained and widgets load accordingly after refresh)
testCases.notes: If persistence is only per session and refresh vs hard reload behaves differently, log exact browser reload type used.

testCases.title: Verify that default landing state behavior works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure the page-level default state loads the expected last-7-days window and that all primary KPIs/charts reflect that scope on first load.
testCases.scenario: Default Landing State Behavior
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to at least one unit
Browser storage/session is cleared for persisted analytics filters
testData: Expected default date format: DD MMM YYYY – DD MMM YYYY
Example default window from mockup: 10 MAR 2026 – 17 MAR 2026

steps.action: Open the SafetyOS Dashboard - Analytics page in a new browser session.
steps.expectedResult: The page loads successfully and the date range input shows the default last-7-days window (e.g., '10 MAR 2026 - 17 MAR 2026') with calendar icon visible.

steps.action: Verify the unit selector shows 'ALL UNITS' by default and is not showing a blank/invalid state.
steps.expectedResult: Unit scope displays 'ALL UNITS' and data appears for the dashboard.

steps.action: Observe the main KPI cards and at least two charts (e.g., Observations Recorded + Timely Closure, plus one chart such as Risk Hotspots or Trend).
steps.expectedResult: KPI counts/percentages and chart visuals render with values consistent with the displayed date window and unit scope.

steps.action: Check the presence of loading placeholders (if any) resolves without leaving widgets blank or partially rendered.
steps.expectedResult: All visible widgets complete rendering for the default scope.

sourceReferences: Source 1: AnalyticsLatest.html (mockup shows default date window and ALL UNITS)
Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 1 acceptance criteria: default last 7 days on first visit)
testCases.notes: This validates page-level readiness and that the default scope is actually applied to initial data render, not just displayed as text.

testCases.title: Verify that future date boundary handling works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure the page-level date picker enforces 'no future dates' and provides the expected tooltip while still applying a valid range to widgets.
testCases.scenario: Future Date Boundary Handling
preconditions: User is logged in
Today’s date is within selectable calendar range
testData: Try selecting a future end date (after today) with a valid start date earlier than today

steps.action: Open the date range picker.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar opens for date selection.

steps.action: Select a valid start date (<= today), then attempt to select an end date after today.
steps.expectedResult: System prevents future end date selection.

steps.action: Hover or inspect the date picker for the tooltip message and then click Apply (if possible).
steps.expectedResult: Tooltip shows 'Future dates are not selectable' and the applied end date is effectively capped at today (dashboard date range reflects today as the end).

steps.action: After applying, confirm widgets reload once with the capped (valid) date range.
steps.expectedResult: All widgets update according to a range whose end is today; no error banner related to invalid end date remains.

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 1: future end date caps at today and displays tooltip 'Future dates are not selectable')
Source 1: AnalyticsLatest.html (tooltip text is referenced by rule evidence)
testCases.notes: Validate both the tooltip and the applied state; the key is that widgets are loaded with a valid date window.

testCases.title: Verify that invalid apply attempt should not trigger cross widget reloads works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure page-level behavior blocks invalid date apply from causing any dashboard data refresh and avoids stale KPI/chart values persisting during an aborted update.
testCases.scenario: Invalid Apply Attempt Should Not Trigger Cross Widget Reloads
preconditions: User is logged in with access to at least one unit.
Analytics dashboard is loaded with the default date window and widgets visible.
testData: Set start date to 11 MAR 2026 and end date to 10 MAR 2026 (end-before-start).

steps.action: Open the shared date range picker calendar from the header filter bar.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar popup opens and the currently applied range is shown as selected/highlighted.

steps.action: Select a start date after the currently selected end date, then select an end date earlier than the selected start date.
steps.expectedResult: The UI prevents selecting an invalid range and shows the inline error 'End date must be after start date'.

steps.action: Attempt to confirm/apply the invalid range (e.g., click Apply/confirmation control in the calendar).
steps.expectedResult: No scope update occurs; no per-widget loading indicators should start.

steps.action: Observe the KPI cards and charts (donut and trend panel) for any change in displayed values or data placeholders while the invalid apply is attempted.
steps.expectedResult: Widget values remain unchanged from the last valid applied range; no stale/partial updates are shown.

sourceReferences: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 1 validation: end-before-start shows inline error and calendar does not close until valid; widgets should reload on valid apply)
AnalyticsLatest.html (presence of KPI cards and charts under shared date window; shows default range formatting)
testCases.notes: If the UI does not expose an explicit 'Apply' within the shared date picker calendar, perform the confirmation attempt using the available control in the calendar popup; success criteria remains 'no reload/no widget loading/no stale values'.

testCases.title: Verify that widgets refresh correctly after filter change.
testCases.objective: Verify that confirm that changing the global date range causes all widgets to reload with loading states and updates consistently (no stale data from the prior window).
testCases.scenario: Widget Refresh After Filter Change
preconditions: User is logged in
Date picker is accessible
Analytics backend is reachable
testData: New valid date range example: 01 MAR 2026 – 15 MAR 2026 (or select any valid custom range)

steps.action: Click the global date range picker in the top filter bar.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar opens and start/end selection UI is available.

steps.action: Select a valid custom date range (start date earlier than end date) and click Apply.
steps.expectedResult: The date range input updates to the newly selected window.

steps.action: Immediately after clicking Apply, observe multiple widgets (e.g., Observations Recorded KPI card, Timely Observation Closure, Ratio of High Risk Observations, SOH%, and at least one chart).
steps.expectedResult: A loading state is shown while new data is fetched; widgets do not remain stuck with old data.

steps.action: After loading completes, compare at least one KPI value before vs. after the change and verify charts also update.
steps.expectedResult: All widgets show values consistent with the new date window; no widget shows a stale value from the prior window.

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 1: applying new date range reloads all widgets with loading state; prevents stale data)
Source 1: AnalyticsLatest.html (shows multiple KPI cards and charts that must update)
testCases.notes: Capture at least one prior KPI value (e.g., Observations Recorded '234') before applying a new range, to verify it changes after reload.

testCases.title: Verify that end date boundary validation handling works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that confirm page-level date validation prevents applying an invalid global range and displays the correct inline error message.
testCases.scenario: End Date Boundary Validation Handling
preconditions: User is logged in
Date picker is accessible
testData: Invalid range: select an end date earlier than the chosen start date

steps.action: Open the date range picker.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar opens and allows selecting start/end dates.

steps.action: Select a start date, then select an end date that is earlier than the start date.
steps.expectedResult: The system prevents forming an invalid range.

steps.action: Look for the inline error message and observe the calendar popup behavior.
steps.expectedResult: Inline error reads exactly (or matches) 'End date must be after start date' and the calendar does not close until a valid range is selected.

steps.action: Attempt to click Apply with the invalid selection (if Apply is enabled).
steps.expectedResult: Apply is blocked or the resulting applied range is not updated; widgets do not reload with invalid scope.

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 1: end date earlier than start date shows inline error 'End date must be after start date' and calendar does not close until valid range selected)
testCases.notes: This is page-level validation behavior for global filters; it must not corrupt widget state.

testCases.title: Verify that the page level experience page level dashboard overview page level behavior supports keyboard navigation readiness across page level controls correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that check page-level accessibility/usability: focus order, ability to open/close date picker, and that tab navigation does not get trapped.
testCases.scenario: Keyboard Navigation Readiness Across Page Level Controls
preconditions: User uses keyboard-only navigation (no mouse)
Accessibility tools available (optional: screen reader)
testData: Keyboard only: Tab/Shift+Tab/Enter/Escape

steps.action: Load the dashboard and press Tab repeatedly from the top of the page.
steps.expectedResult: Focus moves through visible interactive elements in a logical order (header navigation, global filters, then main widgets). No focus trap occurs.

steps.action: Focus the global date range picker and press Enter (or Space) to open it.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar opens; focus is correctly managed inside the popup.

steps.action: Press Escape to close the date picker.
steps.expectedResult: Popup closes and focus returns to the date picker control without losing the previously selected range.

steps.action: Continue Tab navigation to reach a KPI card and at least one chart tooltip/interactive region (if available via keyboard).
steps.expectedResult: Widgets are reachable; there is no broken keyboard interaction or inaccessible overlay blocking focus.

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 1: date picker keyboard/accessibility expectations implied by control behavior)
Source 1: AnalyticsLatest.html (global filters and primary widgets visible at top and in content area)
testCases.notes: This test is page-level and relies on observed keyboard operability; if charts are not keyboard interactive, limit validation to reachability and absence of traps.

testCases.title: Verify that state persists correctly after refresh.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure persisted date selection survives refresh and the reloaded dashboard uses the persisted range for all widgets.
testCases.scenario: State Persistence After Refresh
preconditions: User is logged in
Browser storage/session persistence is enabled
User has previously applied a custom date range via the date picker
testData: Custom persisted range: 01 MAR 2026 – 15 MAR 2026

steps.action: Set the date range to a custom value (e.g., 01 MAR 2026 – 15 MAR 2026) and click Apply.
steps.expectedResult: The date range input reflects the chosen custom window.

steps.action: Refresh the browser (hard refresh if available).
steps.expectedResult: The date range remains set to the previously selected custom window (no reset to default last-7-days).

steps.action: Verify that multiple widgets (at minimum: Observations Recorded, SOH%, and one chart) show values consistent with the persisted range.
steps.expectedResult: All widgets reload using the persisted date range; no mismatch between the displayed date window and widget data.

steps.action: Repeat refresh once more to ensure the persistence is stable across subsequent reloads.
steps.expectedResult: Persisted range remains stable; no oscillation back to default.

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 1: persisted selection retained after refresh; widgets reload with persisted range)
testCases.notes: Persistence is stated as stored per user session (localStorage or server-side session). This test confirms the user-visible behavior.

testCases.title: Verify that resilience to transient api failure after reload works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure resilience: when the analytics data fails to load for the current scope, the page shows safe failure behavior and remains usable.
testCases.scenario: Resilience To Transient API Failure After Reload
preconditions: Test environment can simulate or reproduce a temporary Analytics API failure for the dashboard
testData: Use the default date window and ALL UNITS scope to isolate failure behavior

steps.action: Load the Analytics Dashboard - Analytics page with normal network conditions.
steps.expectedResult: Dashboard renders successfully under stable conditions.

steps.action: Simulate a transient API failure (e.g., block Analytics API response for one reload). Then refresh the page.
steps.expectedResult: The page does not crash; widgets show safe error/loading behavior and the overall layout remains intact.

steps.action: After failure, restore API responses and refresh again (or retry by changing date range back and forth).
steps.expectedResult: Dashboard recovers and widgets render correctly without requiring full browser restart beyond refresh.

steps.action: Confirm that the date range and unit selector state remain consistent with the last-applied scope during failure and recovery.
steps.expectedResult: Filter inputs remain visible and not corrupted (e.g., displayed date range still matches attempted scope).

sourceReferences: Source 2: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (dependencies indicate API-backed UI; page should show safe fallback states without breaking UI)
testCases.notes: Exact inline error messaging for API failures is not evidenced; validate safe failure behavior and recoverability rather than specific copy.

testCases.title: Verify that default range rendering and formatting works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that confirm the Shared Date Range Picker default state and that the visible date range matches the initial data scope across widgets.
testCases.scenario: Default Range Rendering And Formatting
preconditions: User is logged in with access to analytics dashboard.
Start from a fresh browser session where date-range persistence is empty/cleared.
testData: Reference date: today (system date)
Expected default window: today minus 6 days through today

steps.action: Navigate to “SafetyOS Dashboard - Analytics” (BP Whiting - Safety Assistant - Analytics).
steps.expectedResult: The page loads and the Shared Date Range Picker shows a default range.

steps.action: Verify the date range text matches the format DD MMM YYYY - DD MMM YYYY (e.g., 10 MAR 2026 – 17 MAR 2026).
steps.expectedResult: Both start and end dates display in DD MMM YYYY format with the correct delimiter.

steps.action: Confirm the calendar icon and dropdown chevron are visible next to the date text.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar icon appears left of the date text; a chevron appears on the right.

steps.action: Observe at least two dependent widgets (e.g., Observations Recorded KPI and Timely Observation Closure / SOH%).
steps.expectedResult: Their displayed values correspond to the default date window (no placeholder/stale values).

steps.action: Refresh the browser once without changing filters.
steps.expectedResult: The date range display remains the same and widgets do not momentarily display stale counts from a previous range.

sourceReferences: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx - Feature 1 – Date Range Picker with Persistent Selection
AnalyticsLatest.html (mockup shows 10 MAR 2026 - 17 MAR 2026)
testCases.notes: If the date persistence logic resets on first load only, still verify the default window is correct on the first visit after clearing storage.

testCases.title: Verify that maximum range boundary handling works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that validate configured maximum range enforcement at the boundary and ensure dependent widgets react correctly only when a valid range is applied.
testCases.scenario: Maximum Range Boundary Handling
preconditions: User is on Analytics dashboard.
Maximum selectable range is configured (default 90 days) as per spec; if different in environment, use the configured value you observe in UI behavior.
testData: Attempt range = 90 days (valid boundary). Attempt range = 91 days (invalid boundary).

steps.action: Open the Shared Date Range Picker calendar.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar popup opens.

steps.action: Select a start date and an end date exactly at the maximum allowed range (e.g., 90 days including both ends, depending on product definition).
steps.expectedResult: Selection is accepted and highlights correctly.

steps.action: Click “Apply”.
steps.expectedResult: Widgets reload and reflect the selected max-range window without showing stale values.

steps.action: Open the picker again and attempt to extend the end date beyond the maximum range (e.g., 91 days).
steps.expectedResult: System prevents exceeding the configured maximum (via disabled dates, error, or normalization) and does not apply an out-of-range selection.

steps.action: Verify the displayed date range text remains the last successfully applied valid range (if the invalid selection is blocked).

A historical date is currently selected and visible in the input field.
testData: Any valid past date different from the currently selected one.

steps.action: Open the historical-date-picker calendar popup.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar popup opens.

steps.action: Record the currently selected date displayed in the historical-date-picker input.
steps.expectedResult: A specific date value is visible.

steps.action: Click outside the calendar popup area (on a non-interactive part of the page).
steps.expectedResult: Calendar popup closes.

steps.action: Re-check the historical-date-picker input value.
steps.expectedResult: Selected date remains unchanged (no inadvertent update).

sourceReferences: cmn7u57u5000fp73jkavjva36 (Date Range Picker standard: clicking outside closes without changing selected range)
cmn7u57tt000ap73jibtged1c (Date Picker standard: clicking outside closes without changing selected date)
testCases.notes: Directly applicable since historical-date-picker follows single-date picker standards.

testCases.title: Verify that that that the historical date picker supports keyboard accessibility and focus management correctly works correctly works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure historical-date-picker is operable via keyboard and supports predictable focus management.
testCases.scenario: Keyboard Accessibility And Focus Management
preconditions: Analytics Dashboard is loaded.
User can use keyboard (Tab/Shift+Tab, Enter/Space, arrow keys).
testData: Choose an enabled past date using keyboard navigation.

steps.action: Use Tab to move focus to the historical-date-picker input (verify visible focus indicator).
steps.expectedResult: Focus lands on the date input/control.

steps.action: Press Enter or Space to open the calendar popup.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar popup opens.

steps.action: Use arrow keys to move focus between date cells and/or between days/weeks.
steps.expectedResult: Focus moves predictably across enabled/disabled date cells.

steps.action: Select an enabled date using Enter/Space.
steps.expectedResult: Input updates to the selected date.

steps.action: Close the popup using Escape (or keyboard method supported by the UI) or Tab away.
steps.expectedResult: Popup closes and focus returns to a sensible next control without traps.

sourceReferences: cmn7u57tt000ap73jibtged1c (Date Picker standard: date picker is keyboard accessible; focus management for popup)
testCases.notes: If Escape/arrow-key behavior is not supported, report deviation as a gap.

testCases.title: Verify that api failure handling during date change works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that confirm historical-date-picker-driven refresh handles transient failures gracefully and does not break or leave the page in an inconsistent state.
testCases.scenario: API Failure Handling During Date Change
preconditions: Analytics Dashboard is loaded.
Network throttling/tooling available or controlled API failure simulation enabled (if available in test environment).
testData: Select one valid historical past date to trigger refresh with simulated failure.

steps.action: Simulate an API/data failure for the refresh triggered by selecting a historical date (e.g., return error/timeout).
steps.expectedResult: Backend/API fails for the historical-date-scoped query.

steps.action: Change the historical date to a different enabled past date.
steps.expectedResult: Input updates (or remains consistent with UI design), and dependent widgets enter loading state briefly if applicable.

steps.action: Observe the affected widgets after failure.
steps.expectedResult: A safe error state is shown for the failing widget/region (no full dashboard crash).

steps.action: Verify previously displayed stable values are not erroneously shown as if they belong to the new date scope (no stale mix).
steps.expectedResult: Widgets either show error/empty placeholders or previously stable values clearly separated; no inconsistent stale data.

steps.action: Recover the simulated API and re-select (or re-apply) a historical date.
steps.expectedResult: Widgets recover and display correct data for the recovered selection.

tags: scenario-type:api failure handling during date change
sourceReferences: cmn7u57tt000ap73jibtged1c (Date Picker standard: API failures during date changes show safe fallback states without breaking UI)
error_state_region:error-state-regions (inline error regions)
empty_state_region:empty-state-regions (clear empty/no-data messaging)
testCases.notes: Exact UI error message text for this control’s downstream widgets is not evidenced; validate safe fallback behavior and absence of stale corruption.

testCases.title: Verify that calendar navigation and month year rendering works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that validate calendar navigation controls do not unexpectedly change the applied historical date.
testCases.scenario: Calendar Navigation And Month Year Rendering
preconditions: Historical date picker calendar is closed.
testData: A month/year boundary transition (e.g., navigate from December to January or from one month to another).

steps.action: Open the historical-date-picker calendar popup.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar header shows correct current month and year.

steps.action: Click the next month arrow (or prev/next controls) to navigate without selecting a date.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar month/year changes in the popup, but the selected date in the input remains unchanged.

steps.action: Navigate back (reverse arrow) without selecting any new date.
steps.expectedResult: Applied historical date remains unchanged until a new date is clicked.

steps.action: Select a date in the new month view and verify the input updates only after selection.
steps.expectedResult: Input changes to the clicked date.

sourceReferences: cmn7u57tt000ap73jibtged1c (Date Picker standard: navigating between months does not change selected date until a new date is chosen)
testCases.notes: Boundary dates should be selected only if enabled (future disabled).

testCases.title: Verify that future date restriction behavior works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure future dates cannot be selected via the calendar UI for the historical-date-picker control.
testCases.scenario: Future Date Restriction Behavior
preconditions: Analytics Dashboard - Analytics page is loaded successfully.
Calendar popup is closed initially.
testData: A future date after today (relative to system date).

steps.action: Open the calendar popup from the historical-date-picker control.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar popup is displayed.

steps.action: Navigate the calendar to a month containing a known future date (e.g., next month).
steps.expectedResult: Calendar navigation works and the future month is displayed.

steps.action: Attempt to click a future date cell that should be disabled.
steps.expectedResult: Future dates cannot be selected (cell is disabled or click has no effect).

steps.action: Attempt keyboard navigation to focus a future date cell (Tab/arrow keys if supported) and press Enter/Space.
steps.expectedResult: Future dates remain unselectable via keyboard.

steps.action: Verify messaging behavior when attempting a future selection (tooltip or inline message).
steps.expectedResult: If a tooltip/message appears, it states 'Future dates are not selectable' (or an equivalent product-consistent message).

sourceReferences: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 1 acceptance criteria: future end date capped + tooltip; KB standard test cases: today/future disabled when future selection not allowed)
mockup AnalyticsLatest.html (shows future-date tooltip pattern for end date capping)
testCases.notes: Exact tooltip/messaging may differ for historical single-date picker; treat disabled behavior as the primary acceptance criterion.

testCases.title: Verify that default cumulative date window rendering and formatting works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that confirm default cumulative range rendering (format + dates) and that the cumulative widget reflects that default scope on first load.
testCases.feature: Date Control Cumulative Date Range Picker
testCases.scenario: Default Cumulative Date Window Rendering And Formatting
preconditions: User is logged in with access to at least two units (or default ALL UNITS behavior).
User lands on the Analytics dashboard where cumulative analytics widgets are visible.
testData: Default expected range: today minus 6 days through today (inclusive).
Example expected date format: DD MMM YYYY

steps.action: Open the Analytics dashboard and locate the Cumulative Date Range Picker in the cumulative filter area.
steps.expectedResult: The cumulative date range input displays a default one-week window in DD MMM YYYY – DD MMM YYYY format.

steps.action: Verify the cumulative date range input includes a calendar icon/affordance and a chevron indicator.
steps.expectedResult: A calendar icon and a dropdown/chevron affordance are visible for the cumulative date picker.

steps.action: Verify the Cumulative Zone-Wise Split Donut Chart is rendered with zone segments and a legend (if applicable).
steps.expectedResult: The cumulative donut renders and shows values consistent with the default one-week cumulative window.

steps.action: Compare the visible date window text on the cumulative picker with any visible scope labels on the cumulative donut (if present).
steps.expectedResult: The cumulative donut content scope matches the cumulative picker’s displayed default date range.

sourceReferences: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 1 and date picker persistent/default behavior patterns)
AnalyticsLatest.html (default date range display and date input format pattern)
Knowledge base: Date Range Picker and Shared Filtering And Scope Control
testCases.notes: If cumulative picker does not display the same exact formatting as the shared picker, log discrepancy in gaps and adjust expected format accordingly.

testCases.title: Verify that apply workflow refreshes cumulative scoped widgets with loaders and no stale data works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure that changing cumulative date range triggers correct scoped refresh and that cumulative chart values update without displaying stale segments/tooltips.
testCases.feature: Date Control Cumulative Date Range Picker
testCases.scenario: Apply Workflow Refreshes Cumulative Scoped Widgets With Loaders And No Stale Data
preconditions: Cumulative Zone-Wise Split Donut Chart is visible.
Cumulative picker is interactable (not disabled).
testData: Select a valid cumulative date range different from the default (e.g., 01 Mar 2026 – 08 Mar 2026).

steps.action: Click the Cumulative Date Range Picker to open the calendar popup.
steps.expectedResult: The calendar popup opens and allows selecting start and end dates.

steps.action: Select a start date and then a valid end date within the allowed maximum range.
steps.expectedResult: The selected date range is highlighted from start to end.

steps.action: Confirm/apply the selection using the cumulative picker’s apply mechanism (e.g., 'Apply' button if present).
steps.expectedResult: A widget refresh begins; loaders appear for cumulative-scoped widgets.

steps.action: Observe the Cumulative Zone-Wise Split Donut Chart until loading completes.
steps.expectedResult: The donut chart segments/percentages correspond to the newly applied cumulative range; no stale segment values from the previous range are shown after load.

sourceReferences: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 1: widget reactivity + per-widget loading state + no stale data)
AnalyticsLatest.html (cumulative analytics context visible on the dashboard)
testCases.notes: Validate that donut tooltips/legend mapping also update (at least one hover after load) to ensure stale bindings are not retained.

testCases.title: Verify that maximum selectable range boundary handling for cumulative picker works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that confirm cumulative picker blocks range selections that exceed the configured maximum range and does not update widgets with an invalid scope.
testCases.feature: Date Control Cumulative Date Range Picker
testCases.scenario: Maximum Selectable Range Boundary Handling For Cumulative Picker
preconditions: Default maximum selectable range is 90 days (or configured value in environment).
Cumulative picker is available.
testData: Select start date: 01 Jan 2026; attempt end date: 03 Apr 2026 (range > 90 days) when exceeding is possible given calendar.

steps.action: Open the cumulative date picker calendar popup.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar popup opens.

steps.action: Select a start date, then attempt to select an end date that makes the range exceed the maximum selectable range.
steps.expectedResult: The UI prevents the selection or automatically limits the selection to the maximum allowed window.

steps.action: Attempt to apply/confirm the too-long range.
steps.expectedResult: No successful apply occurs for an invalid range; an error or boundary constraint behavior is shown.

steps.action: Confirm that the Cumulative Zone-Wise Split Donut Chart does not change to values corresponding to an invalid range.
steps.expectedResult: Cumulative widget scope remains aligned to the last valid applied cumulative range.

sourceReferences: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 1: Maximum selectable range configurable default 90 days)
Knowledge base: Date Range Picker boundary handling pattern
testCases.notes: Exact UI error copy may differ; focus on behavioral correctness (blocking or capping to max) and no widget scope corruption.

testCases.title: Verify that single day minimal range boundary handling works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure minimal/single-day date range selection is accepted and produces stable cumulative chart rendering.

testCases.scenario: Single Day Minimal Range Boundary Handling
preconditions: Calendar allows selecting a single day for start and end (single-day ranges supported as per date picker patterns).
testData: Select start date = end date: 12 Mar 2026

steps.action: Open the cumulative date picker calendar popup.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar popup opens.

steps.action: Select a single date such that both start and end become the same day (single-day selection).
steps.expectedResult: The input reflects a single-day range or start=end in the displayed range format.

steps.action: Apply the selection.
steps.expectedResult: Cumulative widgets refresh with loaders and then render.

steps.action: Verify the Cumulative Zone-Wise Split Donut Chart displays segments/legend and tooltips remain functional for at least one segment hover.
steps.expectedResult: Chart renders without broken legend/tooltips and shows labels/values for the single-day scoped data.

sourceReferences: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Date picker validation and selection patterns; single-day range highlighted by date picker patterns)
Knowledge base: Date Range Picker single-day range behavior

testCases.title: Verify that end before start validation blocking within cumulative picker works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that confirm cumulative picker validation prevents an invalid range and blocks apply.
testCases.feature: Date Control Cumulative Date Range Picker
testCases.scenario: End Before Start Validation Blocking Within Cumulative Picker
preconditions: Cumulative date picker calendar popup can be opened.
At least one valid date is available for selection.
testData: Pick start date: 08 Mar 2026; pick end date: 07 Mar 2026.

steps.action: Click the Cumulative Date Range Picker to open the calendar popup.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar popup is open.

steps.action: Select a start date (later date) followed by an end date that is earlier.
steps.expectedResult: The UI indicates an invalid selection state.

steps.action: Attempt to apply/confirm the invalid range.
steps.expectedResult: An inline error is shown: 'End date must be after start date' (or the equivalent cumulative picker validation message).

steps.action: Verify the calendar popup remains open until a valid range is selected.
steps.expectedResult: The calendar does not close due to the invalid selection.

sourceReferences: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (Feature 1 validation: 'End date must be after start date' and calendar does not close until valid)
Knowledge base: Date Range Picker validation/error behavior

testCases.title: Verify that future date selection capping and tooltip behavior works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure cumulative picker prevents future dates and communicates restriction via tooltip.
testCases.feature: Date Control Cumulative Date Range Picker
testCases.scenario: Future Date Selection Capping And Tooltip Behavior
preconditions: Today is reachable/known in the test environment calendar.
Future end dates should be restricted by product rules.
testData: Select start date: 01 Mar 2026; attempt end date: 20 Mar 2026 if that is in the future relative to today.

steps.action: Open the cumulative date picker calendar popup.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar popup opens successfully.

steps.action: Select a valid start date in the past and then try to select a future end date.
steps.expectedResult: The future date selection is blocked or normalized to today.

steps.action: Observe the cumulative date range input value after attempting selection.
steps.expectedResult: The end date in the input is capped to today.

steps.action: Hover over or focus the tooltip trigger (if present) that explains the restriction.
steps.expectedResult: Tooltip text is shown: 'Future dates are not selectable'.

testCases.title: Verify that persistence of cumulative selection across browser refresh works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that confirm cumulative picker state persistence and correct widget reload after refresh.
testCases.feature: Date Control Cumulative Date Range Picker
testCases.scenario: Persistence Of Cumulative Selection Across Browser Refresh
preconditions: User has selected a non-default valid cumulative date range previously.
testData: Custom cumulative range example: 01 Mar 2026 – 15 Mar 2026

steps.action: Set the cumulative date picker to a custom range (different from default) and apply it.
steps.expectedResult: Cumulative widgets refresh and reflect the new custom cumulative range.

steps.action: Record the displayed cumulative date range text in the cumulative picker.
steps.expectedResult: Displayed range text matches the applied custom range.

steps.expectedResult: The page reloads and retains the cumulative date range selection.

steps.action: Verify the Cumulative Zone-Wise Split Donut Chart matches the persisted cumulative date range (data and/or visible scope alignment).
steps.expectedResult: Cumulative donut is consistent with the persisted cumulative range; no reset to default one-week occurs.

testCases.title: Verify that that that the date control cumulative date range picker supports keyboard and accessibility behavior for cumulative picker calendar popup correctly works correctly works correctly.

preconditions: Keyboard-only navigation is possible on the page.

steps.action: Use Tab/Shift+Tab to focus the Cumulative Date Range Picker input/control in the cumulative filter area.
steps.expectedResult: The date picker control receives visible focus.

steps.action: Press Enter or Space to open the calendar popup.
steps.expectedResult: Calendar popup opens and focus moves into the calendar in a predictable manner.

steps.action: Navigate the calendar dates using keyboard controls (e.g., arrow keys) and select a start date, then an end date.
