testCases.title: Verify that legend to slice mapping under cumulative scope works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure legend-to-slice mapping is correct and consistent for the cumulative zone-wise split donut chart under a stable cumulative scope.
testCases.feature: Cumulative Zone Wise Split Donut Chart
testCases.scenario: Legend To Slice Mapping Under Cumulative Scope
preconditions: BP Whiting SafetyOS analytics dashboard is open.
Cumulative stats section is visible and the Cumulative Zone-Wise Split Donut Chart has finished initial loading.
Legend for this donut chart is visible (zone labels with corresponding colors).
testData: Cumulative date range in default state (use the page default cumulative window).

steps.action: Inspect the legend entries for each zone and note the displayed label(s) and/or numeric value(s) shown alongside the color markers.
steps.expectedResult: Each legend entry is present and visually distinct (different color marker per zone).

steps.action: Hover over one specific donut segment (slice) for a zone.
steps.expectedResult: The tooltip (or focus highlight) indicates the same zone label as the hovered legend entry.

steps.action: Move the hover across at least 2 different segments (different colors/zones).
steps.expectedResult: Each hovered segment consistently corresponds to the correct legend entry (no swapped colors/labels).

steps.action: Verify the sum/relationship displayed by the chart for proportions is not contradictory to legend values (e.g., if legend shows percentages, ensure tooltip values align with that same scheme).
steps.expectedResult: Legend values and tooltip values for corresponding zones follow the same basis (no mismatch like percentage shown in legend but count shown in tooltip for the same zone).

sourceReferences: AnalyticsLatest.html (zone wise split donut and legend presence)
Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (cumulative analytics widgets + general chart behaviors)
testCases.notes: If tooltip shows only label/value but legend shows only percentages (or vice versa), verify the mapping at least by label and color; avoid strict numeric equality if formats differ.

testCases.title: Verify that cumulative scope isolation vs page level shared filters works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure cumulative picker scope isolation: only cumulative zone-wise donut updates, while page-level KPIs and other charts remain scoped to page-level Shared Date Range Picker.
testCases.feature: Cumulative Zone Wise Split Donut Chart
testCases.scenario: Cumulative Scope Isolation Vs Page Level Shared Filters
preconditions: Cumulative Date Range Picker is visible.
Cumulative Zone-Wise Split Donut Chart is visible.
testData: Page-level date range A: 10 MAR 2026 - 17 MAR 2026
Cumulative date range B: choose a clearly different one (e.g., 03 MAR 2026 - 09 MAR 2026)

steps.action: Set page-level Shared Date Range Picker to Date range A.
steps.expectedResult: Page-level KPI cards and distribution summary chart reflect Date range A.

steps.action: Record current values (or labels) from at least two page-level KPIs (e.g., Observations Recorded count and SOH%).
steps.expectedResult: Baseline page-level KPI values are noted for later comparison.

steps.action: Change Cumulative Date Range Picker to Date range B and apply.
steps.expectedResult: Cumulative Zone-Wise Split Donut Chart refreshes and shows updated segment percentages/counts for Date range B.

steps.action: Verify page-level KPIs (Observations Recorded, Timely Closure, High Risk Ratio, SOH%) do not change.
steps.expectedResult: Page-level KPIs remain scoped to Date range A (no unintended cumulative influence).

steps.action: Hover a cumulative donut segment to show tooltip and confirm it aligns to the cumulative refresh.
steps.expectedResult: Tooltip values match the refreshed cumulative donut, not the previous cumulative range.

sourceReferences: Source 2 (Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx): cumulative date range picker affects cumulative analytics; shared filtering drives dashboard widgets
testCases.notes: If cumulative picker is intended to influence page-level widgets as well, document that behavior and adjust expected results in review.

testCases.title: Verify that empty zero bucket rendering safety for cumulative zone donut works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that ensure the cumulative zone-wise split donut chart handles a zero/none dataset safely without broken legend, broken hover/tooltips, or layout collapse.
testCases.feature: Cumulative Zone Wise Split Donut Chart
testCases.scenario: Empty Zero Bucket Rendering Safety For Cumulative Zone Donut
preconditions: Cumulative date range picker can be adjusted.
User can reach the cumulative zone-wise split donut chart on the page.
testData: Cumulative range expected to produce no qualifying zone split data (select a recent minimal range that likely yields zero). If deterministic test data is unavailable, use a range that demonstrates an empty chart state in the environment.

steps.action: Set the cumulative date range to the test range that yields no qualifying data (or use a range where the donut chart is observed to display no segments).
steps.expectedResult: Chart updates and shows an explicit empty/no-data state (no crashed visualization).

steps.action: Verify the legend area does not display misleading zone/value pairs (e.g., no incorrect percentages/labels for nonexistent segments).
steps.expectedResult: Legend either shows no entries or clearly indicates no data, and does not show stale previous legend values.

steps.action: Attempt to hover where a segment would be (or attempt focus on chart areas if focus is supported).
steps.expectedResult: Tooltips do not show stale tooltip content; if no segments exist, tooltips should not appear with old values.

steps.action: Return to a non-empty cumulative date range (previous known good range) and confirm the chart restores correct segments.
steps.expectedResult: Donut segments, legend entries, and tooltips repopulate correctly for the new scope.

sourceReferences: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (overall dashboard widgets must reload safely and show empty/no-data states)
AnalyticsLatest.html (donut chart and legend visual areas; general chart behavior expectations)
testCases.notes: If the UI keeps legend entries but sets their values to zero, validate that values are zeroed and not stale rather than expecting legend entries to disappear.

testCases.title: Verify that malformed payload resilience for cumulative zone split works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that confirm that malformed/invalid data returned for cumulative zone split does not break rendering and provides a safe fallback state.
testCases.feature: Cumulative Zone Wise Split Donut Chart
testCases.scenario: Malformed Payload Resilience For Cumulative Zone Split
preconditions: Ability to simulate/force malformed API response for the cumulative zone-wise donut chart in the test environment (e.g., via QA backend stub, test toggle, or known failing payload scenario).
Cumulative stats section is visible.
testData: Malformed response payload for zone split (e.g., missing required fields like zone label, null numeric values, or invalid percentage formatting).

steps.action: Trigger a cumulative date range change that causes the cumulative zone-wise split donut chart to request fresh data.
steps.expectedResult: Chart enters a loading state for the donut widget.

steps.action: Ensure the API returns a malformed payload for this specific donut chart request (while other widgets may remain functional).
steps.expectedResult: Donut chart does not crash the page and does not render corrupted SVG/canvas elements.

steps.action: Observe the donut chart area and legend/tooltip behavior after the malformed response.
steps.expectedResult: Chart shows a safe error/fallback UI or empty/no-data UI; legend does not show misleading/stale values; tooltips do not show incorrect or unparseable content.

steps.action: Return to a valid cumulative date range (restore a known-good dataset).
steps.expectedResult: Cumulative donut chart recovers and renders correct segments/legend/tooltip behavior again.

sourceReferences: Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (widgets must reload on date range change with loading states; invalid data must not break UI)
AnalyticsLatest.html (chart layout areas that must remain stable even when data is missing)
testCases.notes: Exact fallback messaging is not evidenced; validate that the UI remains stable, avoids broken legend/tooltips, and recovers after returning to good data.

testCases.title: Verify that tooltip content and keyboard focus behavior on donut slices works correctly.
testCases.objective: Verify that validate keyboard-accessible tooltip behavior for donut slices and confirm tooltips do not show stale content after the cumulative scope changes.
testCases.feature: Cumulative Zone Wise Split Donut Chart
testCases.scenario: Tooltip Content And Keyboard Focus Behavior On Donut Slices
preconditions: Cumulative Zone-Wise Split Donut Chart is visible and interactive.
User can use keyboard to navigate to chart interactive elements (tab/shift-tab).
A cumulative date range change is available (via the cumulative date range picker control elsewhere on the page).
testData: Cumulative range A (default) and cumulative range B (select a different cumulative start/end window).

steps.action: Tab through the page until the donut chart interactive elements receive focus (e.g., slice focus ring or a chart focus indicator).
steps.expectedResult: Donut slice focus is reachable via keyboard with a visible focus indicator.

steps.action: With focus on a donut slice, check that a tooltip appears (or an equivalent accessible tooltip/aria-described content becomes visible).
steps.expectedResult: Tooltip content is displayed/announced and corresponds to the focused slice.

steps.action: Press Tab/Shift+Tab to move focus between at least two different slices.
steps.expectedResult: Tooltip content updates to match the currently focused slice; no cross-slice leakage occurs.

steps.action: Change the cumulative date range to a different window (apply the new cumulative range and wait for loading to complete).
steps.expectedResult: Donut chart re-renders for the new range; tooltip content shown afterward reflects the new dataset (no stale values from the prior range).

sourceReferences: AnalyticsLatest.html (donut chart and tooltip system implied by hover/tooltip behaviors)
Analytics_Dashboard_User_Stories.docx (cumulative analytics widgets and overall dashboard reactivity)
testCases.notes: If chart slices are not focusable and tooltip is hover-only, mark as a gap/defect for accessibility expectations; still validate re-scope non-stale behavior via hover after range change.

testCases.title: Verify that default cumulative scope rendering and legend slice mapping works correctly.

testCases.scenario: Default Cumulative Scope Rendering And Legend Slice Mapping
preconditions: User is logged in and can access the BP Whiting SafetyOS Dashboard analytics page.
Cumulative Date Range Picker is available on the page and has a default cumulative range applied.
testData: Default cumulative date range (as shown on initial load).

steps.action: Locate the Cumulative Zone-Wise Split Donut Chart section and the adjacent legend.
steps.expectedResult: The donut chart displays multiple zone segments and a legend list appears showing zones.

steps.action: Compare each legend zone label to the corresponding donut segment color.
steps.expectedResult: Each legend item color matches exactly one donut segment, with no missing legend entries for visible segments.

steps.action: Visually verify segment ordering/number of segments is consistent with the legend count.
steps.expectedResult: The number of visible donut segments matches the number of legend entries.

steps.action: Hover each segment once (without changing filters).
steps.expectedResult: All segments respond with hover feedback (tooltip will be validated in a dedicated case). No segment appears non-interactive.

testCases.scenario: Tooltip Hover Behavior On Cumulative Donut Slices
preconditions: Cumulative donut chart is visible and the legend is rendered.
Tooltips are supported for donut segments.
testData: Switch cumulative date range to a different valid range (e.g., move the start/end by at least 1 day).

steps.action: Hover a specific donut slice (e.g., a zone segment visible in the chart).
steps.expectedResult: A tooltip appears showing the hovered zone label and its associated value/percentage consistent with the legend for that zone.

steps.action: Move the mouse away from the slice and then hover a different slice.
steps.expectedResult: The tooltip updates to the newly hovered zone (no mismatch to the previously hovered zone).

steps.action: Change the cumulative date range using the Cumulative Date Range Picker and apply/confirm the new cumulative range.
steps.expectedResult: The chart shows a loading state during refresh (validated more explicitly in the loading case).

steps.action: After the new data renders, hover the same zone slice again.
steps.expectedResult: Tooltip content reflects the new cumulative scope (zone label still correct; values/percentages updated to match the new legend/slice). No stale tooltip content from the previous scope is shown.

testCases.scenario: Cross Widget Reconciliation Of Cumulative Scope (consistency Checks)
preconditions: At least two widgets are visible whose metrics are driven by the cumulative date range and unit scope (e.g., KPI cards such as SOH% or risk-related cards, if they update with the cumulative picker).
testData: Two distinct cumulative date ranges (Range A and Range B).

steps.action: Capture donut legend zone values/percentages for Range A (at least one zone).
steps.expectedResult: Donut legend is visible with zone mapping for Range A.

steps.action: Identify the KPI/card(s) that change according to the same cumulative scope (based on observed UI behavior) and capture their displayed key numbers for Range A.
steps.expectedResult: Selected cross-widget metrics reflect Range A.

steps.action: Switch to Range B via the Cumulative Date Range Picker and wait for charts/cards to finish reloading.
steps.expectedResult: Donut and referenced widgets update to Range B without stale values.

steps.action: Confirm that donut tooltip/legend values for Range B correspond to the newly displayed KPI/card context (no obvious reconciliation mismatch such as percent signs swapped, zones stale, or percentages not aligning with the visible totals implied by other widgets).
steps.expectedResult: Cross-widget scope appears consistent: donut and other widgets reflect the same date scope changes without desynchronization artifacts.

testCases.title: Verify that legend rendering resilience when chart data partially loads or fails works correctly.

testCases.scenario: Legend Rendering Resilience When Chart Data Partially Loads Or Fails
preconditions: A way to trigger reload of donut/chart data via scope change (date range or unit selection).
The environment can reproduce a transient API/data failure or partial load for chart payloads (via test harness, throttling, or controlled backend fault).
testData: Scenario A: simulate transient failure for donut chart data response associated with legend.
Scenario B: simulate delayed legend mapping data vs donut segment render (if separable).

steps.action: Initiate a scope change (change date range and/or unit selection) that triggers chart reload for the donut area tied to this legend.
steps.expectedResult: Legend and donut area show loading behavior appropriate to the product (no stale mismatch visible immediately).

steps.action: During loading, observe the legend values and entries.
steps.expectedResult: Legend does not change to a different mapping than the in-progress donut; if legend updates are delayed, it should either stay consistent with the previous stable state until the new data is ready or show a loading/placeholder state—no mixed legend/data states.

steps.action: After the donut finishes loading successfully, compare legend entries again to the donut segments.
steps.expectedResult: Legend values/colors match the final rendered donut segments for the same scope; stale legend from the previous scope is not left behind.

steps.action: If a chart error occurs (for the associated donut feed), verify legend does not remain in a misleading partially-rendered state.
steps.expectedResult: On error, legend either displays an empty/error-safe state or remains non-interactive without incorrect mapping; UI does not crash.
